summary: Frontend testing for dynamic site settings integration. All dynamic contact info (phone, email, HR email, social links) correctly uses useSiteSettings hook. Fixed Next.js build cache issue for Projects page Quick View button CSS.
critical_code_review_comments: All components correctly use useSiteSettings() hook for dynamic content
Contact page shows dynamic phone (+971 50 326 7225) and email (info@lexalifestyle.com) from API
Careers page shows dynamic HR email (careers@lexalifestyle.com)
Work-with-us page Apply Now buttons use dynamic HR email in mailto links
Footer social links correctly render from site settings API
Header logo uses settings.header_logo_light/dark with proper fallbacks
Quick View button on Projects page now uses correct centering CSS (left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2)
Blog page Quick View already had correct centering CSS
seed_data_creation: None - used existing site settings data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dynamic site settings integration complete. useSiteSettings hook provides: contact_phone, contact_email, hr_email, social links, and logos. API endpoint: /api/site-settings. Build cache was an issue - if CSS changes don't appear, run: rm -rf .next && yarn build

site_settings_api.GET /api/site-settings: PASS - Returns all settings including contact_phone, contact_email, hr_email, social links

contact_page.contact_form: PASS - Form loads with all fields

careers_page.company_phone: PASS - Shows company phone from settings

work_with_us_page.dynamic_hr_email: PASS - Email CV button uses careers@lexalifestyle.com
work_with_us_page.apply_now_buttons: PASS - Job cards use dynamic HR email in mailto links

footer.social_links: PASS - All social links (Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook, TikTok, Twitter/X, WhatsApp) render from settings

consultation_form.modal_opens: PASS - Form opens when Book Consultation clicked

projects_page.quick_view_button_css: PASS - Now uses left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 for stable centering

blog_page.quick_view_button_css: PASS - Uses left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 for stable centering

build_fix_applied.issue: Next.js build cache was serving old CSS for Projects page Quick View button
build_fix_applied.fix: Ran rm -rf .next && yarn build to force full rebuild
build_fix_applied.result: Button now uses correct left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 centering instead of old inset-x-0 mx-auto
